content: Just as 2025's configurable Intrinsic Curiosity Module rewards AI autonomous agents for exploring uncharted swarm UAV territory to identify novel targets, biological agents (e.g., soil microbes or immune cells) in natural ecosystems gain evolutionary fitness advantages from their 'intrinsic curiosity' for undercolonized niches, rather than only competing for already crowded, resource-dense areas. This parallel suggests that evolutionary biologists could adapt the ICM framework to model and predict how natural microbial agents spread and explore new environments.
